prompt:
Passage 1
A regional warehouse recently introduced a new system for tracking incoming deliveries. In the past, receiving clerks recorded shipments on paper forms and later entered the information into a computer. This process sometimes caused delays because paperwork could be incomplete, misplaced, or entered incorrectly.
The new system allows clerks to scan delivery documents as soon as shipments arrive. Information is automatically uploaded to the warehouse database, where supervisors can review delivery times, quantities, and supplier information. Managers report that records are now available more quickly and that fewer corrections are needed.
The warehouse also uses the data to identify suppliers that frequently deliver late or send incomplete orders. By reviewing this information, managers can address recurring problems and improve planning.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The warehouse stopped accepting incoming deliveries.
choices.B: A new tracking system improved delivery recordkeeping.
choices.C: Receiving clerks no longer check shipments.
choices.D: Suppliers now control the warehouse database.

explanation: The passage focuses on how the warehouse improved delivery recordkeeping by replacing paper forms with a scanning system that uploads information directly to a database.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning January 8, all employees must complete the updated safety orientation before using the new loading dock equipment. The orientation includes a brief classroom review and a supervised equipment demonstration.
The updated orientation was created after supervisors observed that some employees were unfamiliar with the new controls. The goal is to ensure that all workers understand proper operating procedures before working independently.
Employees should schedule their orientation through the training office. Supervisors will verify completion before assigning employees to loading dock duties.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce a required safety orientation
choices.B: To advertise a new warehouse position

explanation: The notice announces that employees must complete an updated safety orientation before using the new loading dock equipment.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many businesses use inventory forecasts to estimate future product demand. A forecast may be based on past sales, seasonal patterns, customer orders, and current market conditions. Although forecasts are not always exact, they can help companies make better decisions about purchasing and production.
If a company orders too much inventory, it may spend money storing products that do not sell quickly. If it orders too little, it may run out of popular items and disappoint customers. A useful forecast helps businesses balance these risks.
Managers often review forecasts regularly and adjust them when new information becomes available. This process allows companies to respond more effectively to changing conditions.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Inventory forecasts help businesses plan for future demand.
choices.B: Companies should avoid using past sales information.
choices.D: Customers prefer businesses with no inventory.

explanation: The passage explains how inventory forecasts help businesses estimate future product demand and make better purchasing and production decisions.

prompt:
Passage 4
The development of public water treatment systems greatly improved health and daily life in many communities. Before these systems were common, people often relied on wells, rivers, or other local sources that could become contaminated. Unsafe water could spread disease and make communities more vulnerable to illness.
Water treatment systems use several steps to remove harmful materials and reduce health risks. These steps may include filtering, settling, and disinfecting the water before it reaches homes and businesses.
Today, public water treatment remains an essential service. Engineers and utility workers continue to monitor systems, test water quality, and maintain equipment to keep water supplies reliable.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Public water treatment systems improved community health and reliability.
choices.B: Communities no longer need water testing.
choices.C: Wells are always safer than treated water.
choices.D: Water treatment systems are used only in factories.

explanation: The passage describes how public water treatment systems reduced health risks and continue to help keep water supplies reliable.

prompt:
Passage 5
A company that repairs industrial equipment recently introduced a customer service portal. Before the portal was launched, customers usually called the service desk to request repairs, ask about job status, or submit equipment information. During busy periods, callers sometimes waited longer than expected.
The new portal allows customers to submit service requests online, upload photos of equipment problems, and check the status of existing jobs. Service managers report that the portal has reduced call volume and helped technicians receive clearer information before arriving at a job site.
The company continues to offer phone support for customers who prefer it, but leaders expect online usage to grow as customers become more comfortable with the system.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company stopped repairing industrial equipment.
choices.B: A customer service portal improved repair-request handling.
choices.C: Customers are no longer allowed to call the service desk.
choices.D: Technicians now complete all repairs remotely.

explanation: The passage focuses on how the customer service portal improved the way repair requests are submitted, tracked, and handled.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many workplaces use checklists to support consistent performance. A checklist may list required steps for opening a facility, inspecting equipment, preparing materials, or closing a work area at the end of a shift.
Checklists are useful because they reduce reliance on memory. Even experienced workers can forget a step when they are tired, interrupted, or working under time pressure. A clear checklist helps employees confirm that important tasks have been completed.
However, a checklist is only helpful when it is accurate and actually used. Supervisors may review checklists periodically to make sure they still match current procedures.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Checklists can improve consistency when they are accurate and used properly.
choices.B: Experienced workers never need written instructions.
choices.D: Checklists are useful only for new employees.

explanation: The passage explains that checklists support consistent performance, but only if they are accurate and actually used.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities use stormwater drainage systems to manage rainwater runoff. When rain falls on roads, parking lots, and rooftops, the water cannot always soak naturally into the ground. Instead, it flows across hard surfaces and must be directed away from streets and buildings.
Drainage systems may include gutters, storm drains, pipes, retention ponds, and channels. These systems help reduce flooding and limit damage during heavy rainfall. They also help prevent water from collecting in areas where it could create safety hazards.
Regular maintenance is important because leaves, trash, and sediment can block drainage pathways. When systems are not maintained, even moderate rain can cause problems.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: How stormwater drainage systems manage runoff and prevent problems
choices.B: Why communities should remove all paved surfaces
choices.C: How rainwater is used to generate electricity

explanation: The passage explains what stormwater drainage systems do, why they are needed, and how maintenance helps prevent flooding and safety problems.

prompt:
Passage 8
A manufacturing company recently reviewed its employee suggestion program. The program allows workers to submit ideas for improving safety, reducing waste, or making production more efficient.
During the review, managers found that several employee suggestions had led to useful changes. One team proposed reorganizing frequently used tools closer to workstations, which reduced unnecessary walking time. Another employee suggested clearer labels for storage bins, helping workers locate materials more quickly.
After reviewing the results, company leaders decided to continue the program and provide quarterly updates about suggestions that are adopted. They believe this will encourage more employees to participate.

What is the purpose of the employee suggestion program?

choices.A: To allow workers to submit improvement ideas
choices.B: To replace supervisors

explanation: The employee suggestion program allows workers to submit ideas for improving safety, reducing waste, or improving efficiency.
